title: Block Internal Requests From the Preview Fetcher
objective: Patch the preview fetcher to block requests to internal and metadata addresses.
briefing: Logs show the preview endpoint was used to reach the cloud metadata service and expose credentials. The fetcher needs to stop trusting arbitrary destination addresses.
hints: Restarting apache2 doesn't add a destination check that doesn't exist.
The fix needs to reject requests to internal-only address ranges before they're made.
debrief: The patch adds a destination check before the server makes any outbound request on the user's behalf: reject RFC1918 private ranges, loopback, link-local addresses, and specifically 169.254.169.254 (the cloud metadata address). This directly fixes the SSRF (A10:2021) by refusing to let a public-facing feature reach internal-only infrastructure. Defense in depth would add an allowlist of expected destination domains/schemes and, on the cloud side, requiring IMDSv2's token-bound requests so metadata access needs more than a bare GET even if a future SSRF slips through.

logs: [SSRF] preview endpoint used to reach internal metadata service - credentials exposed

patch /var/www/api/preview.php --block-i.output:
patched /var/www/api/preview.php
blocking RFC1918, loopback, link-local, and 169.254.169.254... done

logs_append: [FIX] preview fetcher now blocks RFC1918 and 169.254.169.254 metadata requests
